title: Hamnet Wins Best Motion Picture Drama at 2026 Golden Globes
text: Director Chloé Zhao's film Hamnet claimed the top drama prize at the Golden Globes ceremony held Sunday evening. The Focus Features production triumphed over competitors including Frankenstein, It Was Just an Accident, The Secret Agent, Sentimental Value, and Sinners. Producer Steven Spielberg presented remarks during the acceptance, explaining that he believed Zhao was uniquely suited to adapt Maggie O'Farrell's novel. He stated: there was really only one filmmaker on the face of the planet who could tell the story. Zhao delivered an emotional speech referencing comments actor Paul Mescal had made to her earlier that day about vulnerability in artistic work. She praised her fellow nominees for their openness, saying: you have shared so much of yourselves in your work to the world, so I salute to your bravery. The film's star Jessie Buckley, who plays Agnes, had won her own award moments earlier for best female actor in a drama.
